title: pilosella roxolanica subsp. rubricymigera
intro: Pilosella roxolanica subsp. rubricymigera is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the aster family, characterized by its red-tipped leaves. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, due to its purgative effects. The health benefits of Pilosella roxolanica subsp. rubricymigera include its ability to stimulate bowel movements, reduce inflammation, and act as an antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a dried herb or in infusion form, and can also be used to make tinctures and infusions for internal use.
